display_name: High TCAM utilization reflects configured feature scale — it only matters when programming fails
context: Reviewing hardware TCAM/ACL resource utilization on a switch and seeing one or more regions at high percentage
observation: A TCAM region (ACL, QoS, routing, or PBR) shows high utilization, e.g. 85-100% in one carve-out
naive_interpretation: The device is about to fail and TCAM must be expanded or the switch replaced immediately
correct_interpretation: TCAM is statically carved into regions sized by the configured feature profile, and utilization simply reflects how many entries that feature is using. A region at high utilization is fine as long as new entries still program successfully. The real failure signal is a TCAM/resource-exhaustion log or an entry that fails to install (silently software-switched or dropped), not the percentage alone. Also, one region can be exhausted while overall TCAM looks low, because the carve-outs are independent.
why: TCAM is partitioned into fixed regions at boot per the hardware/feature profile; utilization is per-region, not a single pool
A region at 90% is harmless if the feature is stable and not adding entries; the danger is only at the moment an install is attempted and fails
Exhaustion manifests as explicit log events (e.g. ACL/FIB TCAM full) or entries falling back to software/CPU forwarding, which is the actionable symptom
Aggregate TCAM percentage hides per-region exhaustion — the relevant number is the specific carve-out the feature uses

confirmation_commands.purpose: Show per-region TCAM utilization to find the specific carve-out that is full rather than the aggregate
Look for explicit resource-exhaustion or entry-install-failure events, the real signal of impact

confirmation_commands.command: show platform hardware fed switch active fwd-asic resource tcam utilization
confirmation_commands.purpose: Per-region TCAM utilization on Catalyst platforms to identify the exhausted partition

traps: Panicking over a high percentage in one region when entries still program fine and no exhaustion has been logged
Reading the aggregate TCAM figure and missing that a single region is actually full
Not checking whether failed installs caused silent software/CPU forwarding, which is the genuine impact
Re-carving TCAM regions (which requires a reload) without confirming which region is actually constrained
